headline: FT webinar: what does the Budget mean for your money?
summary: FT journalists and tax expert Dan Neidle answered your questions after the 2025 Budget
body_text:
Investors and savers in the UK had been anxiously awaiting clarity on which taxes chancellor Rachel Reeves would raise in the 26 November Autumn Budget as she sought to repair public finances.

On 28 November, Financial Times journalists and expert guest Dan Neidle met to unpack her announcements and answer audience questions.

The panel, moderated by consumer editor Claer Barrett with investment columnist Stuart Kirk and leader writer Tej Parikh explored the Budget’s implications for households and investors. They assessed how the rule changes will affect personal finances, influence tax-planning strategies and their potential impact on investments, including property.

The webinar will also be broadcast as a bonus episode of two FT podcasts: Claer’s Money Clinic, and the weekly UK politics show Political Fix.
